LHX.sentence: L3Harris is up a bit today and looking interesting after falling 18% from its highs — defense spending keeps growing, and it's the cheapest of the big defense contractors.

ASML.sentence: ASML had a solid day, up about 5% — nothing dramatic happening, just the usual steady climb for the company that makes the only machines capable of printing the most advanced chips.

LIN.sentence: Linde is quiet, up just over 1% — the industrial gas business is steady as always, though their profit margins aren't quite as fat as they used to be.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is down about 3% and now sitting 30% below its peak, which is rare territory for a stock this dominant — the AI infrastructure story is still intact, just getting cheaper to own.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is down a bit as biotech companies are still working through excess inventory from the pandemic boom, but that's expected to bottom out by early 2026.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is holding steady — the pipeline business keeps collecting its tolls, though bond yields are getting high enough that some investors are choosing Treasury bonds over the dividend.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow is down after falling 51% from its peak, which is extreme even for a software stock — the AI workflow automation business is still growing, just got very expensive and is now correcting.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore is down slightly as copper prices stay soft, but the mining and trading giant tends to make money in volatile commodity markets regardless of direction.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is up a bit today but still sitting about 29% below its moving average — after the big run-up, this kind of cooling-off period is actually healthy.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is up nearly 2% — the peso exchange rate is steady, and Latin American soda consumption keeps chugging along.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is up slightly but still trading well below recent levels — oil prices are cooperating, but the stock has been in a funk for a few months.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk had a strong day, up nearly 5% — the shipping giant is trading at a big discount to what it would cost to replace its fleet of container ships.
